campaign: Key Conversations to Help Protect Autonomy and Assets
venue: The Capital Grille, 9005 Mercato Drive, Naples, FL 34108
co_presenters: Brandon Beck, Wealth Manager (Confluence FP) + Ehren Frey, Associate Attorney (Zacharia Brown & Bratkovich)

ads.title: Brand introduction image
ads.primary_text:
Most people who come to our workshops already have a financial plan. They come to find out if it's actually coordinated.

On Wednesday, May 14, fiduciary wealth manager Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are hosting a complimentary lunch at The Capital Grille in Naples. The focus: the gaps that show up between your investment plan, your estate documents, and your tax strategy when nobody's looking at them together.

Bring your spouse. This one works better as a couple.

24 seats. No follow-up calls. No obligation.
ads.headline: Is Your Plan Coordinated?
ads.hook: Most people who come already have a plan. They come to find out if it's coordinated.

ads.title: Educational carousel (5 cards)
ads.primary_text:
You've done the planning. But when was the last time your estate attorney, your tax advisor, and your wealth manager were in the same conversation?

On Wednesday, May 14 at The Capital Grille in Naples, Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are sitting down together to walk through the four areas where plans tend to fall out of sync. Complimentary lunch included.

24 seats. Couples welcome.
ads.headline: Where Plans Fall Out of Sync
ads.hook: You've done the planning. But when was the last time all your advisors were in the same conversation?

cards.label: Hook
cards.caption: You have an investment advisor. You have estate documents. You probably have a tax strategy. The question is whether they're working together — or just sitting in different folders.

cards.label: Control & Autonomy
cards.caption: If something happened tomorrow, does your family know who steps in? Do your documents say what you actually want them to say — or what you wanted five years ago?

cards.label: Roth Conversions
cards.caption: Roth conversions have a window. Converting in the right year versus the wrong year can change your tax bill by thousands. Timing matters more than most people realize.

cards.label: Legacy
cards.caption: Your heirs shouldn't have to guess your intentions. We'll talk about putting wishes in writing and how charitable giving fits the bigger picture.

cards.label: CTA
cards.caption:
Wednesday, May 14 at 12:00 PM
The Capital Grille, Naples
Complimentary lunch. 24 seats. Couples welcome.
Reserve yours.

ads.production: Brandon Beck direct-to-camera, filmed in office. Lapel mic. Captions mandatory.
ads.primary_text:
I host these workshops a few times a year. The people who show up aren't starting from scratch — they've already done the planning. What surprises most of them is how much has drifted since they last looked at everything together.

On Wednesday, May 14, I'm sitting down with estate planning attorney Ehren Frey at The Capital Grille in Naples. Complimentary lunch. We'll cover Roth conversion timing, autonomy documents, and how families are thinking about legacy and charitable giving.

Bring your spouse. No follow-up afterward. I'd be glad to meet you there.

You chose Naples for a reason. You built a life here worth taking care of.

The financial decisions that matter most right now aren't about returns. They're about coordination: does your family know who steps in if you can't make decisions? Do your estate documents still reflect what you actually want? Is your Roth conversion timing aligned with your tax picture?

On Wednesday, May 14, fiduciary wealth manager Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are hosting a complimentary lunch at The Capital Grille. This is for couples and individuals who've already done the work and want to make sure the pieces still fit together.

24 seats. No follow-up calls. No obligation.

ads.title: Infographic / visual concept
ads.primary_text:
There's a real difference between having a financial plan and having a coordinated one.

With coordination: your estate documents name the people you trust, your Roth conversions are timed with your full tax picture in mind, and your heirs aren't left guessing.

Without it: a court may choose who manages your affairs. Tax timing works against you on distributions. Your family sorts it out under pressure.

On Wednesday, May 14, Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey are hosting a complimentary lunch at The Capital Grille in Naples. The workshop covers what a coordinated plan looks like and the questions worth asking. Couples encouraged.
ads.headline: Planned vs. Uncoordinated
ads.hook: There's a real difference between having a plan and having a coordinated one.
ads.infographic_direction:
Left column, "Coordinated plan":
- Trusted decision-makers named in advance
- Roth conversions timed with your tax picture
- Estate documents up to date and working together
- Heirs prepared and informed
- Charitable giving structured for income and legacy

Right column, "Without coordination":
- Court-appointed decision-makers
- Tax timing working against your distributions
- Outdated or conflicting estate documents
- Family left guessing
- Charitable intentions unfulfilled

ads.title: Expert insight video
ads.production: Brandon at desk or conference room. Conversational, not scripted. Lapel mic. Captions mandatory.
ads.primary_text:
Roth conversion timing is something people tend to set and forget. But the right year versus the wrong year can mean a significant difference in your tax bill — and for a lot of people in retirement, the window is narrowing.

It's one of several topics we'll cover at our complimentary lunch on Wednesday, May 14 at The Capital Grille in Naples. Fiduciary wealth manager Brandon Beck and estate planning attorney Ehren Frey. Couples welcome.
ads.headline: A Question of Timing
ads.hook: Roth conversion timing is something people tend to set and forget.

stories.text_overlay: "The Roth conversion timing question most people set and forget"

ads.video_script:
1. Open with the topic, not yourself: "Here's something people tend to set and forget: the timing on Roth conversions."
2. Middle: "The right year versus the wrong year can mean a significant difference in your tax bill. For a lot of people in retirement, the window is narrowing."
3. Bridge: "This is one of several things we'll cover at our complimentary lunch on May 14 at The Capital Grille. Couples welcome."
4. Close: "Details below. I think you'll be glad you came."
